entry.title: Re: SAC and Group Reporting Implementation Timing
entry.content:
Hello Sen,

Thank you for your quick response. I appreciate the information. I have a follow-up question regarding the statement that GR and SAC might experience a 'technical go-live' before the actual data flow.

To ensure I understand correctly, could you please clarify whether this 'technical go-live' refers to:

A deployment of GR and SAC before the SAP S/4HANA core goes live?
Parallel deployment of GR and SAC while the SAP S/4HANA core is already in production.
Furthermore, considering that we are not migrating historical data, I'm concerned about the potential impact on GR and SAC if they go live in parallel with S/4HANA. It seems that GR and SAC would require initial data, such as loaded balances or current transactions, from the S/4HANA core to function effectively. Could you please elaborate on how this scenario would be handled?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025
entry.content:
@GregBotticchio It was forgotten to mention that a new license key is required for BI 2025. Also, this is not mentioned in the installation or upgrade guide, nor in any other guides or articles.
It seems like this was a last-minute decision to implement the new license routine.  or is it a software error in handling the existing BI 4.3 license?

entry.title: Re: Express Document "Update Was Terminated" Received fro...
entry.content:
Basically, when you receive an  "Update Was Terminated" message, execute SM13 to find the exact FM that triggered it during update. 

Often this is a print form error, and the FM or Dump will indicate Smart Forms  or Adobe: Generated Function Group Name (Form name in text of FG)

  • /1BCDWB/SF* for Smart Forms
  • /1BCDWB/SM* for Adobe Forms

entry.title: Active Ingredient Management in S/4HANA Cloud Public edition.
entry.content:
Hi,

Where can we find information on Active INgredienat Management of products? We manufacture products which have two units of measure  - litres and Litres of absolute alcohol. Each batch manufactured and sold requires both measures. The one is a percentage of the other (LAA is around 96.4% of L)

 

We will need to use these in production, batch management, stcok valuation as well as sales.

 

Many thanks

Rob

entry.title: FSCM CM: Merge Tax - SDCM/MM flow
entry.content:
Hi experts, 

Even tought I have flagged field MERGE_TAX in VFCLM_FB_CHN_STP customizing, items from MM and SD are been load in FQM_FLOW in 2 flow:

SDCM:  900000 Incoming Bank Cash and 900018 Incoming Bank Cash (Tax)

MM: 900001 Outgoing Bank Cash and 900019 Outgoing Bank Cash (Tax)

I've already applied SAP note: 3499992 - Flow Builder Plus: Merge tax items based on tax codes.

Has anyone faced this scenario? Could you please help me?

Thanks in advance.

 

BR

 

RL

entry.title: Re: Joule, SAP AI-copilot Available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in Early Adopter Care
entry.content: Hi is the Joule available in 3SL s4hana cloud public edition? and GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition basic ?

entry.title: Source of Supply Assignment differs for MRP and manually created Purchase Requisitions
entry.content:
Does anyone have more details on an issue we experienced in S4 Hana where the source of supply determination seems to work differently when manually creating PReqs vs PReqs created via MRP?
We are trying to implement simplified sourcing (eliminating the use of source lists) and have the MRP pick up a contract directly to be assigned to the created PReqs.
Expectation is (same as with using source lists) that source assignment is looking for a valid contract which is valid at the time of the delivery date specified in the PReq.
This is valid when manually creating the PReqs via ME51N.
However, when MRP creates the PReqs it is always the PReq creation date which determines the contract to be selected.

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
entry.content: The configuration link is no more active!

entry.title: Re: Pop-up alert option in SuccessFactors
entry.content: Hi there, we still do not have a solution. I forgot that I did create an enhancement request. Please consider voting! https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/258383/?section=sectionDetails

entry.title: what is the maximum number of lines we can create for a contract?
entry.content:
Hi SAP experts,

kindly help in information related to the maximum number of lines allowed to create a contract in SAP HANA?
Client is having 18,000-line items for a contract in their Legacy System. How to manage this situation kindly help me.

Thanks,

Divya

entry.title: camt.053.001.02 Memo lines extracted from the wrong tab
entry.content:
Hi,

We are importing CAMT-format bank files from Liquidity Management - Inbound Files into our ByDesign test tenant.

I have configured the ERP to accept the "ISO 20022 Account Statement" format. However, the memo lines generated in the bank statement are being extracted from the following tags:

<AcctSvcrRef>ZZ1HXWB80X4EWEZ5X</AcctSvcrRef>

instead of using the memo lines found within these tags:

<RmtInf> <Ustrd>INVOICE 110 2025/20250217</Ustrd> </RmtInf>

Is there a way to configure the system to extract this information from a specific tag?

This issue is causing the loss of important information. We need to be able to configure the system correctly to ensure the memo lines are captured accurately.

Thanks in advance for your help!

Elen

entry.title: Re: Calculating Energy Consumption in the Product Carbon Footprint
entry.content:
Hello,

We addressed this challenge in the first phase by recording energy consumption and allocating the emissions with an Allocation Scheme. In the second phase, we added Energy Flow Models down to the production lines to improve the specificity of the calculation. Now, the emissions are allocated via the use of production lines (Internal Service Confirmation).

I don’t think there is a way to extract the allocated and calculated energy consumption per product. Isn't the goal to determine the product's carbon footprint anyway?

 

Klaus

entry.title: Re: Runtime Errors MESSAGE_TYPE_X & ABAP: Program CL_FINS...
entry.content:
Read first 3235247 - [FINSC_LEDGER] Unique Currency Types Namespace exceeded and create an Issue 

  • "If this problem is occurring in your productive client, please create an SAP support case (component FI-GL-GL) and refer to this note."

entry.title: Repricing at VF04
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a requirement where I want to do repricing at VF04 (collective billing) after clicking on save. how to do repricing (any function modules or any piece of code) and any enhancements available to achieve this. Please share your inputs.

Thanks,

Ravi

entry.title: Re: Inable to create a 3rd party order for a network order/PS Project
entry.content: Please show more details on the other tabs for the procurement settings and any configuration. What messages pop up when you do an Intermediate Save.

entry.title: Re: Calculating Energy Consumption in the Product Carbon Footprint
entry.content:
Hi Again,

Yes in the CCF calculation, you can freely choose which GHG scope to use. 
For PCF you have to use Allocations, Energy Flow Models or Planned Energy Consumptions. You can find some info about those here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/sustainability-footprint-management/application-help-for-corporate-and-products/managing-facilities

entry.title: Re: SAP PS Activity Error Encountered During Period Closing
entry.content: During what closing step are you getting the error. Please provide more details on the steps you are following. And where is the APPROVED status?

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now and Signavio Integration Error
entry.content:
Hi dominic_michaelraj, 

this seems to be a specific issue that appears in your account and might be related to your / your organizations settings or setup. It is not a known error. 

Thus, please open a support ticket to have an expert look into this specific issue. You can open a support ticket in SAP for Me as described in this video. 

Many thanks and best wishes, 

Katie

entry.title: Clarification on Solman Application for SAP Satellite/Managed Systems in SAP HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Solman and SAP Cloud Experts,

I would like to clarification about SAP Solution Manager (Solman) applications for SAP Satellite/Managed Systems in SAP HANA Private Cloud. 

Activities for SAP Satellite/Managed Systems Migrating to SAP HANA Private Cloud:

In the context of SAP Solution Manager on-premises, and SAP Satellite/Managed systems migrating or already migrated to the SAP HANA private cloud, could you provide clarity on the activities that need to be carried out? Specifically, I am interested in systems using Solman applications such as ChaRM, SolDoc, EWA, SysMon, DVM, etc. What steps should be taken if transport requests exist in change requests, change documents, or phase cycles with old system entries? How should we proceed in such cases?

SAP Diagnostics Agent Requirements:

Is it necessary to install the SAP Diagnostics Agent and establish connectivity to the Solution Manager on-premises to configure Managed System Configuration for SAP Satellite/Managed systems in the SAP HANA private cloud?

Installation and Access for SAP Diagnostics Agent:

If the SAP Diagnostics Agent needs to be installed at the OS level for both the SAP Application and SAP HANA database in the cloud, who would have access to the OS level at the SAP HANA private cloud, and who would be responsible for installing and connecting it to the Solution Manager on-premises?

Configuration of Solman Applications:

Is it possible to configure Solman applications such as EWA, Technical Monitoring, and DVM for SAP Satellite/Managed systems in the SAP HANA private cloud?

EWA Configuration to SAP Portal:

Can EWA be configured directly to the SAP Portal for SAP Satellite/Managed systems in the SAP HANA private cloud?

I would greatly appreciate your guidance on these matters. Please let me know if it would be more appropriate to submit an OSS ticket, or if there are any other forums or resources that could assist in addressing these queries.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Regards

Srinivas V.

entry.title: Convert canvas with jsPDF and upload to SAP, but corrupted file
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a custom Fiori Application with canvas, which I convert with jsPDF to pdf format and uploading to SAP with the standard oData Service API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/AttachmentContentSet.

I am able to open the PDF in a new browser tab and also successfully can see the file attached to the order in SAP, but when I try to open the file from SAP, it is corrupted. Also downloading it and trying to open with Adobe is not working.

 

 

 

const pdfGenerator = new jsPDF();
pdfGenerator .addImage(oImage,'JPEG',0,0);
const pdfOutput = pdfGenerator.output('datauri'); //datauristring
const pdfPayload = pdfOutput.split(,)[1];

 

 

 

Then I am sending the pdfPayload in the create method of the AttachmentContentSet as payload. 
What am I doing wrong?

Regards,
Georgi

SAPUI5 SAP Business Accelerator Hub 

@DK007

entry.title: Accelerating IT Landscape Insights: SAP LeanIX Integration Into SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
As we continue our series on unveiling EA ADM artifacts, it’s important to start with a solid foundation using SAP LeanIX before diving into key artifacts like the business strategy map,capability map, solution context, and concept. SAP LeanIX will play a crucial role in organizing and presenting these artifacts, ensuring both efficiency and alignment throughout the process.

In the TOGAF methodology, capturing the "as-is" application architecture is crucial as it provides organizations with a clear view of their current systems, processes, and technologies, helping identify inefficiencies, risks, and gaps. This step serves as the foundation for creating a roadmap for future improvements.

For customers using SAP products, we’ll streamline this process with SAP LeanIX’s landscape discovery feature, offering a comprehensive view of the SAP landscape while minimizing manual effort and improving data accuracy.

The SAP landscape discovery in SAP LeanIX uses SAP Cloud ALM to gather up-to-date landscape information, ensuring data accuracy.

Once the "as-is" SAP landscape is captured, we will prepare the Baseline Solution Architecture by identifying relevant applications and IT components impacting our business objective of improving customer satisfaction. We’ll also analyze dependencies between these components, how they interact, and map out users and roles in the Solution Context Diagram and Stakeholder Matrix, providing a complete view of the architecture.

Below are the steps for integrating SAP Cloud ALM with SAP LeanIX for auto-discovering systems and services: 

Current Limitation and Prerequisites

  • The SAP landscape discovery feature currently supports SaaS products, SAP On-Premise systems, and ERP systems. Discovery of SAP Private Cloud and BTP services is upcoming (see roadmap and here )
  • Admin access in SAP LeanIX and BTP Cockpit access for the CALM account are required.
  • If CALM is subscribed and the user has proper authorizations, services and systems, including registered on-premise SAP NetWeaver systems, can be viewed in SAP Cloud ALM's landscape management
  • For multiple subsidiaries or customer numbers, create a corporate group for additional registrations

Create a new API instance for LeanIX Integration

Create the new service instance with below the parameters accessing landscape data in CALM

{
"xs-security": {
"xsappname": "<your-instance-name>",
"authorities": [
"$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.landscape.read",
"$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.subscriptions.read"]
} }

 Create Service Instance with Proper JSON Parameters

 Create the service key

Generate the key and, if possible, validate it here

Create the Service key

 

Add SAP Landscape Discovery integrations

In the SAP LeanIX administration section, go to the Integrations tab, select "Add Integrations,"

Add Integrations

find "SAP Landscape Discovery," and choose "Configure."

SAP Landscape Discovery

Give name for the integrations

Name for Integration

Based on your needs, either select or unselect SaaS and on-premise product discovery.

Select the options for Product discovery

  Copy the JSON service key from the SAP BTP cockpit that you created earlier.

Copy JSON

 enter the keys under "Authenticate via API." Click "Save and view the status.

Data Sync in process

 we can see the sync logs by View Sync Logging. Daily Sync Status

 Details of the loggingSync Logging

Discovery Inbox

After the integration is successfully configured, the discovery process will identify your SAP cloud products and display them in the SAP Landscape Discovery Inbox.

Discovery Inbox

In the inbox, you can examine the discovered items and either link them to existing fact sheets or create new fact sheets and associate them with the items Link to relevant existing factsheets from SAP RBA

 Thus SAP landscape discovery feature in SAP LeanIX automatically gathers data about your SAP systems and services. It provides a clear view of your current SAP landscape, reducing manual work, filling in gaps, and improving data accuracy. This speeds up the discovery process during ERP transformations and makes updating your application landscape easier and more automatic as it changes. In our next blog, we will proceed with the business strategy map.

And don’t miss out—our next blog will explore the business strategy map, and it's something you’ll want to see!

entry.title: BOM Status 02 consider in program CS_BOM_PRODVER_MIGRATION02
entry.content:
In S4HANA system,  in program - CS_BOM_PRODVER_MIGRATION02, BOM status not equal to 2  but still production version is created for the BOM with status 02 (inactive).

Material has two alternative BOM (1, 2), Alt BOM 1 has BOM status 2 and Alt BOM 2 has BOM Status 1, but valid date and Lot size are same .

When program is run, it has consider both BOM for prod version. Why program is doing that.

Kindly reply.

entry.title: Re: Dumps while executing tcode /OTX/PF03_WP
entry.content: You should create a case on component XX-PART-OPT-INV similar to the one described in 2864721 - SAP Invoice Management by Open Text - dump for TA /OTX/PF03_WP.

entry.title: Re: Refreshing the Consumption Item Mapping when a Field...
entry.content:
Hi Eric, thanks for the reply. Does it mean that we need to unlink any charge where this chargeable item class is linked and delete the old one, and then create a new one? Is there any cookbook or documentation that we can refer to? We need to clearly explain to the customer the situation and having a documentation as base for our proposal would be the best

Thank you, Doris

entry.title: Re: Refreshing the Consumption Item Mapping when a Field...
entry.content: Hi Eric, thanks for the reply. Does it mean that we need to unlink any charge where this chargeable item class is linked and delete the old one, and then create a new one? Is there any cookbook or documentation where we can refer to? Thank you, Doris

entry.title: Re: Generative AI with SAP – Part 1: Introduction to SAP Business AI
entry.content: Very helpful article Raj! 🙂

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.1: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.1 is now available.

To access the final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.1 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access it.

For you as a customer, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.1 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

You can use the commenting feature for What’s New. With this feature, authors can evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: Re: Does the SAP Enable Now Certification will be retired...
entry.content: Hi @HoangAnhNguyen in my opinion at this time I won't recommend to pursuit the EN certification as it will be retired in April and you will hold it only for one year then you won't be able to renew it so for me it doesn't make sense as the tool will be replaced completely by WalkMe so I'd rather wait for the deliver of the product under SAP then pursuit this certification on the SAP Learning Hub portal.

entry.title: When Does an S/4HANA Implementation Become a Business Transformation?
entry.content:
Many people in the software industry will refer to an ERP implementation, for example, an SAP S/4HANA implementation, as a “business transformation."

Does an ERP implementation equate to a business transformation? Not necessarily. I would argue that a technical upgrade, sometimes called a “lift and shift,” is an ERP implementation but can hardly be called a business transformation since, from a business standpoint, little or nothing changes or "transforms."

Sometimes, companies elect to do a “brownfield” ERP implementation with the driving objective to minimize change for the users and avoid possible business disruption. Once again, I cannot call this a business transformation as they are attempting to minimize any changes to their operational business processes.

On the other hand, an ERP implementation project can be the perfect vehicle for making significant company changes with the purpose of better positioning and equipping the company for the future.

Why have your software company representatives not conveyed a valid vision or approach for business transformation? To be blunt, most software people represent a particular product area within their companies. The S/4HANA people will talk about the benefits of S/4HANA. The BTP people will talk about the benefits of BTP. The business process management people will talk about the benefits of Signavio and Lean IX. The Ariba people will talk about the capabilities and benefits of Ariba. And so on.

The key point is that very few software people will talk holistically about transforming your business. A holistic approach is realistic and, by far, the most beneficial and practical. Why not implement a new, more powerful, more capable enterprise system and ALSO address other high-impact aspects of your company? All under the umbrella of “ERP Implementation.”

An ERP implementation will provide benefits, especially in the areas of IT costs and complexity. However, there are other very significant benefits to be had by expanding the initiative to include other IT aspects, business aspects, and joint business-IT aspects.

The following list summarizes some of the opportunities and benefits of an ERP implementation project and of a business transformation project. The list is, to a degree, in order of the most obvious to least obvious, but all are very practical and realistic for both IT AND business improvements:

1. Reduce IT costs

Benefit:  Reduce cost of computer hardware, data center, cyber-security, etc.

Retire purchased and home-grown legacy applications by deploying a broader ERP footprint. Fewer applications also means fewer interfaces to maintain. Consider moving on-prem applications to the cloud to reduce “data center” expenses. The tools provided by ERP (e.g., SAP Business Technology Platform) allow for “tailoring” the new system to the specific business needs of your company for more streamlined processes. This cost reduction benefit is often the primary component of an ERP replacement/upgrade “business case,” although many other more difficult-to-quantify benefits should also be considered.

2. Reduce IT complexity

Benefit:  Encourage operational innovation and make the company more nimble.

Along with the cost-reduction points above, a simpler IT landscape with fewer applications and interfaces makes ongoing and future business process changes easier. Whether the change is to tweak a sales order process to make it more efficient or to roll out an entirely new business model, reduced IT complexity will make change easier. How often have process changes been avoided due to the fear of breaking things? How often have new processes been avoided because of the complex IT environment that currently does not support the new processes?

Simplifying your IT landscape should include a “clean core” initiative to move any required customizations outside of S/4HANA. S/4HANA with BTP are designed for developing and maintaining customizations in a manner that will not interfere with future upgrades and other system changes.

3. Lay the IT foundation for the future

Benefit:  Ability to leverage new technologies as needed, when needed to innovate and keep up with competition.

A major benefit of new ERP systems is the leveraging of the most current information technologies.  In-memory processing has revolutionized enterprise systems and enables the execution of an operational digital twin of your company’s operations, which in turn enables interactive applications (i.e., user alerts). 

More recently, the rapid development of applications enabled by Artificial Intelligence (AI) promises a sea change in how business processes are performed and how business intelligence is generated and delivered. Data collection methods continue to evolve, helping ensure complete capture of large volumes of high-quality, real-time transactional data. Running your company on old ERP systems will almost certainly impair your ability to compete as other companies design and adopt new processes and provide customers with improved service methods.

Companies remaining on ECC are already suffering from not being able to deploy new AI-enabled processes and other enhanced capabilities (i.e., workflows, Fiori apps, etc.) only available for S/4HANA.

4. Improve operational business processes

Transformation Benefit:  More effective and efficient business processes, contributing to lower cost, higher profitability, and better customer service and relationships.

This sounds simple and obvious, but companies struggle with it. The “this is how we’ve always done it” mindset makes it difficult to initiate process changes. Some companies are still using business processes they established decades ago based upon the technology at the time. However, they are running them on much newer, and much more capable, technology attributable to “technical upgrades.”

New systems have new capabilities that need to be considered. You might be able to decommission applications or remove older customizations. Automation of previously manual business processes and role-specific user cockpits can improve operational performance with minimal incremental cost. Capabilities for process automation, role-specific cockpits, and user interactivity are available in S/4HANA and BTP.

The HANA in-memory database has a major advantage over older disk-based databases. Faster processing enables the elimination of batch processes and the capture of more transactions and transaction details. S/4HANA has the power to run genuinely as your operational digital twin. Combined with new, improved data collection methods, S/4HANA will deliver more high-quality information to those who need it for daily decision-making.

5. Enhance your Business Process Management (BPM) program

Transformation Benefit:  Ability to manage company processes and operational performance.

Managing business processes is far more than documenting how work is currently done.

Operational business performance relies on the determination of processes capable of achieving desired performance objectives. Yet, many companies neglect to establish standard, acceptable processes and then monitor the execution of those processes to ensure conformance.

Just as in the world of manufacturing, process variations can result in poor or inconsistent quality and additional costs. There is no reason to rely on luck and hope to achieve performance goals. Becoming more of a process-oriented company is the only reasonable option.

In the context of your ERP implementation, your implementation team should identify and implement operational processes that are capable of achieving acceptable results. Digital platforms like SAP Signavio Process Manager are available to facilitate collaborative process design. This allows people in separate geographies to more effectively work together to identify processes that satisfy everyone’s needs (when possible). This collaboration, along with digitally-enabled communication, will help with employee acceptance and reduce resistance to change.

However, the bigger BPM program opportunity comes from the ability to analyze process execution to objectively assess employee conformance and performance. Process mining, provided by SAP Signavio, provides the ability to determine where process variations are occurring and helps assess the financial impact and impact on customer service of those variations. This has revolutionized how process managers govern business processes and orchestrate process changes.

After months of determining the right business processes for your company and building them into your new ERP, you will want to use process mining to monitor actual process execution to identify performance and conformance issues.

6. Adopt data-driven operational management

Transformation Benefit:  Faster, earlier, and better business decisions at all levels.

The best business decisions are made when decision-makers have the data or information they need to make the right decisions.

S/4HANA provides more opportunities for collecting and storing more data and more granular transactional data for real-time visibility and automated notifications using situation handling. This means that people at every level of the organization can access the information they need when they need it. It also opens the opportunity for information being pushed to the right people for proactive management of events and circumstances that offer a business opportunity or a business threat.

The sooner someone knows about an opportunity or a threat, the sooner they can take action to maximize the positive impact on the company.

Additional analytics and alerts based upon your company’s specific needs are feasible through the use of SAP BTP for process automation and SAP Signavio for process mining that can trigger company-specific alerts. SAP Analytics provides the foundation for data-driven operational management.

The tailoring of company-specific analytics, triggers, alerts, and automations should be part of your S/4HANA implementation to get the most value from your system. These capabilities will also be used on an ongoing basis for new process improvements after ERP go-live.

7. Enhance your Continuous Improvement program (business)

Transformation Benefit:  Maximizing the impact of your continuous improvement resources to improve customer interactions, profitability, and employee adoption of new processes.

Many companies today have something they call their “continuous improvement” program. Frequently, this is a person or group that is instructed by management to address business issues such as customer complaints, employee complaints, profitability leakages, chronic transactional errors, and a myriad of other issues.

A common weakness of many existing programs is the reliance on subjective criteria (i.e., opinions, biases, politics) for evaluating process performance and identifying improvement targets. These programs are almost entirely reactive, with the company’s limited “improvement” resources working on the wrong things while bigger issues go unnoticed or undetected.

Business process analytics (i.e., process mining) capabilities have progressed tremendously over the past few years and provide process managers with objective process performance measurements to help ensure the best improvement opportunities are identified, often proactively, by a continuous improvement leader.

Further, collaborative process design capabilities allow many employees and process stakeholders to participate effectively in enhancing the processes. This greatly reduces resistance to change and helps identify, develop, and deploy the best new processes.

An ERP implementation is the perfect time to rethink the company’s Continuous Improvement program. The methodology and tools used to improve processes during the implementation will be the same methodology and tools used within the Continuous Improvement program post-implementation.

8. Update your approach to strategic data management

Transformation Benefit:  Enhanced decision support.

Modern companies are data-driven. The days of little data and decisions based upon gut feel, intuition, opinions, selective perceptions, biases, and political forces should be a thing of the past. Today’s technology is far more capable of gathering great volumes of data and turning it into valuable information and insights.

In the context of ERP implementation, it is important to ensure the system has high-quality data for processing and that high-quality data is generated for decision support.

But the world of data is moving quickly. In addition to ERP data or data from multiple ERP systems, companies also have access to customer data (CRM), plant floor data (MES), machine telematics data (internal and external), purchased industry forecast data, economic forecast data, customer demand forecasts, and many other types of data. Capturing, consolidating, and making it consumable by business management has never been more practical.

Technology for managing all of this data is available and rapidly evolving. It is realistic to think the most successful companies are the ones that best determine how to effectively combine and utilize all of this data. The data management capabilities under the SAP BTP umbrella need to be considered for consolidation, federation, and governance of your corporate data.

Your ERP implementation is the opportune time to rethink your data strategy for ERP as well as beyond ERP. The current data explosion is more than a fad. The best companies will likely be the companies that best manage and consume the available data.

9. Develop an enterprise-wide approach to Organization Change Management (OCM)

Transformation Benefit:  Better, faster, and less costly adoption of ongoing company changes in processes, business models, organization, technology, etc.

Everyone agrees that change is a fact of life and business and that the rate of change is always increasing. Companies that fail to change or fail to manage change will likely fail.

ERP implementation methodologies are heavy on the technical aspects of implementation, but fall short when it comes to change management. The usual emphasis is on system configuration, data cleansing and migration, interfaces, custom code, etc. Aside from employee training near the end of the implementation project, relatively little focus is on the human element of the system change.

“Change Management” has consistently been identified in CIO surveys as one of the top three challenges encountered when implementing a new ERP, including S/4HANA. Proactively addressing change management as part of your implementation is imperative for reducing resistance to change and boosting user acceptance.

But it does not end there. Your company needs to become skilled at executing change. A strategic initiative is to make “change management” a company core competency, even to the point of making it part of the company culture, where everyone is encouraged to suggest, promote, and participate in change activities.

As mentioned, a planned approach to change management is crucial for your ERP implementation. The methods, practices, people, processes, and tools of change management need to continue after ERP go-live. Company leaders and managers need to be trained on the company’s OCM (Organizational Change Management) methodology, guided in their use of the methodology, and monitored for compliance. It is that important.

The training aspect of your ERP project is extremely important. Training methods and solutions have also come a long way over the past few years. SAP Enable Now should be considered as a foundation for project ERP training and ongoing training and retraining.

10. Reorganize or adjust for the future

Transformation Benefit:  An organizational structure that better represents and supports where the company is heading.

Most company organizational structures are based upon their past. Your company’s strategy might justify adjustments of the structure or possibly a complete reorganization.

An extreme example of this is the number of CIOs who report to CFOs. In the distant past, the primary purpose of IT was to support the financial reporting function of the company, so having IT report to the CFO made good sense. Today, IT support is needed by the entire enterprise and should be much more strategic for future business. Yet, many CIOs continue to report to the financial leadership.

Your ERP implementation project is a great opportunity to consider and execute organizational changes. Your company is already dealing with changes and hopefully has a better mindset for understanding and accepting the changes. A key aspect of making these changes is to provide an honest rationale to the people impacted, which includes why the change is appropriate or required and how the new organization will be better.

 

You can do an S/4HANA implementation for the IT benefits, or you can transform your business, beyond IT, to better support your current and future corporate strategy. An ERP implementation project is a great opportunity to make some changes, and maybe some very overdue changes, to how you manage and prepare your company for success.

An ERP implementation project can be a terrible thing to waste! It is your choice AND your opportunity!

The information and opinions written here are the views of the author. Any comments or feedback can be sent to Chet Harter at chet@chertharter.com

Visit my website at chetharter.com

entry.title: Re: InteractiveDonutChart doesn't show less than 3 segments
entry.content: Hi, I've found it's solved when I use the FlexBox. It allows to define the minimum size of the chart, then when there is only one or two segments, it still follows the minimum size of the FlexBox.

entry.title: Re: Does the SAP Enable Now Certification will be retired...
entry.content: Yes @Lluis it seems we need to wait for more information on the deliverable of the product, the education and certifications under SAP as for WalkMe right now they have 3 certifications in the portal but most probably all of this will be migrated to the SAP pages.

entry.title: Best Approach for Changing Connection from Quality to Production in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello everyone,  
 
I would like your advice on the following issue. We have a customer with only one SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) tenant, the production one, without a test environment.  
 
We started implementing some dashboards using S/4 data, based on the Quality system connection. As a result, we built the data models and corresponding stories using the Quality connection.  
 
Now that we are in the UAT phase, we are wondering what the best approach would be to change the connection from Quality to Production without having to create the models and stories from scratch. Ultimately, we aim to have two versions of the stories, one with Quality data and another with Production data.  
 
Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated!  
 
Thank you in advance.
SAP Analytics Cloud, connectivity

entry.title: Re: Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025
entry.content: We as SAP Partner can't see the option to ask for a license key for any of our customers that have BusinessObjects and we can't demo this new version to any of our customers without a (emergency) key...

entry.title: Re: First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher)
entry.content:
Hi,
for a landscape S4 Hana Rise I would like to start with SAP Cloud ALM for Change & Deployment Scenario.

With Solution Manager CHARM there is the CSOL functionality that ensures that when an object is changed in a managed system, it is locked in the SAP Solution Manager system. Depending on the conflict scenario, this prevents multiple edits of the same object in different transport requests or projects at the same time. This applies to all managed systems and clients for which the cross-system lock is active.
This allow maintenance line and evolution line to be under control.

I've read that CSOL functionality for SAP CALM will be available but only for detect whether development objects have been touched in different system landscapes; this is not my case because I have only one S4 Hana Landscape.

So if I decide to use CALM how can I manage conflict between evolution line and maintenance line if CSOL it's not released for this purpose?

Which are the best practices to follow for managing evolution and maintenance line with features to replicate similar functionality?

Regards
Maurizio

entry.title: Re: Intercompany STO EWM Outbound delivery - Account assi...
entry.content:
Hello together, 

we also face the same issue. Does someone have an answer, how this can be solved?

Regards

entry.title: SAP BTP FinOps Application as a SaaS offering ?
entry.content: As per the session, the FinOps application is not a SaaS service, but need to be deployed by each individual customer/organization. Why is it developed that way and not as a SaaS offering. Does SAP have plans to make FinOps into a SaaS service for customers to subscribe to and use?

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, Integration Edition: Configuration Settings and Implementa
entry.content:
Hi ,

Thank you for putting this together .It will be very helpful for customers , as these are most common issues in IAG projects.Few questions and suggestions here :

1) PAM - Integration edition provides PAM function now ?If not , we should remove that brief section from this blog as it titles int edition. Otherwise, it can miss-lead.

2) As mentioned in blog  "Workflow Management: Disable the mitigation control assignment approval workflow when the BRIDGE scenario is active" . Does that means customer can not use Assignment approval workflow at all now ?Do we have this already in IAG int edt or planned ? 

3) As mentioned "Access Synchronization in Business Role Management:utilize the ‘SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Role Import’ option during repository synchronization .Do not use the ‘Role Import’ utility from the NWBC launchpad for cloud access.

Sync jobs sometime dont import roles in BRM ,even after selecting this check box. Using NWBC role import can create some issue? What to do if roles are not imported in BRM even after this option was selected during sync ?

4) Multiple times term  "Cloud Connector" is used for IAG (cloud)connected systems. We also have Cloud Connector as product (reverse proxy to OP). This term here again can create confusion. eg. "Include the cloud connector created in SAP Access Control within the connector group"  or"repository synchronization jobs for cloud connectors"

Thanks,

Uppdeep Mann

entry.title: Issue in Overall release of PR workflow WS20000077
entry.content: when we approve PR from My inbox app using workflow WS20000077 , its working fine for L1 approver . and FRGZU updated with X but when the final level approver approves it field FRGZU is resetted to X  instead of XX so final approver comes as blank . if we do manually from ME54n , Its working fine .

entry.title: Re: Layering of Key User Extensibility and Developer Extensibility
entry.content:
Hi Thomas,

this documentation describes to use a separate software component for T1 developer extensibility.

Set Up Developer Extensibility | SAP Help Portal

Which software component we should use for key user extensibility e.g. custom fields?

1. We recognized that only HOME packages can be used in app "Configure Software Packages". Is that right?
2. The generated data element of the custom field needs to be C1 released otherwise an error is shown by using the fields in CDS within a T1 development. 

Thanks for your help.

Regards Wolfgang

entry.title: Re: Multiple plants in MRP list/Stock List
entry.content:
Try MMBE, if this can help

Kind regrds

entry.title: Need visibility into determination of KF Codes present in Asset Balances report (F1617)
entry.content:
We have certain asset-related GL accounts that we've added to the chart of accounts, and thus which aren't present in the Asset Balances report (Link to Fiori app library page ). This report utilizes certain Key Figure codes to show column breakouts of depreciation, acquisition, etc., each of which appear to align with mutually exclusive GL accounts, with Net Book Value being a grouping of all GL accounts.

In tracing the CDS view used by this report (C_ASSETBALANCE within FINS_FIS_AA_APPS), I'm having a difficult time finding exactly what table(s) or other items are used to define the KF Codes presented in this report. Can someone point me in the right direction for seeing how the KF codes are defined? As an example, KF code 000014-0010790401 is NBV at reporting Date (see below). Ideally, I'd like to explore adding certain GL accounts to be included in these KF codes. Note that I'm relatively new to a technical role, so apologies if there's an obvious T-code I'm missing.

Thank you

entry.title: Count total amount of a dimension
entry.content:
Hello! I'm trying to create a graph in our team story, where I need to calculate the average of the score performance of each member of the team, and for that, I need to count the amount of times the owner repeats in the data, but the counting dimensions option doesn't work for me and I'm trying to find a way to do this. 

Also I will appreciate if someone can help me in a way if it's possible to have a format condition like in Excel where with a condition, I can assign a color to the bars of a graph.

entry.title: FCTechDays2025 – Your Conference for SQL Anywhere Migration
entry.content:
We’re back—this time fully virtual for our international audience!

Conference details: https://www.appfact.de/konferenz/techdays2025

Why SQL Anywhere Migration?
SQL Anywhere has long been one of the most popular and powerful database systems, helping countless developers enhance their internal and industry-specific solutions.
But now, SQL Anywhere is facing the end: Unlike MS SQL and PostgreSQL, it’s not available as a DBaaS solution, and support will officially end in 2028.
Ironically, SQL Anywhere's biggest strength—its powerful and flexible SQL dialect—is now its biggest challenge, significantly complicating migration to other database systems.
With our innovative tool "dbmAIgrate" we offer a practical migration solution which makes use of AI technologies.
At this event, you'll also learn how to build your own AI system and gain basic AI programming skills.
We’ll demonstrate the migration practically with Microsoft SQL Server—but we’re also happy to support migrations to PostgreSQL or MySQL upon request.

Secure your tickets now at:
👉 https://appfact.de/konferenz/tickets

(German version below)

Wir starten erneut – dieses Mal virtuell für unsere internationalen Kunden.

Infos zur Konferenz: https://www.appfact.de/konferenz/techdays2025

Warum SQL Anywhere Migration?
SQL Anywhere ist eines der beliebtesten und leistungsfähigsten Datenbanksysteme am Markt und hat Generationen von EntwicklerInnen geholfen, ihre Inhouse- und Branchenlösungen auf ein neues Level zu heben.
Doch nun fällt dieses bewährte System der Cloud zum Opfer, denn SQL Anywhere steht nicht als DBaaS-Lösung zur Verfügung. Im Gegensatz zu MS SQL und PostgreSQL ist 2028 endgültig Schluss mit SQL Anywhere.
Gerade die Stärke von SQL Anywhere, der leistungsstarke und flexible SQL-Dialekt, wird nun zum Verhängnis, da er eine Migration zu anderen Datenbanken extrem erschwert.
Mit unserem innovativen Tool „dbmAIgrate“ zeigen wir Ihnen einen praktikablen Weg zur Migration auf, die auf KI-Technologien basiert.
Zusätzlich lernen Sie, wie Sie ein KI-System aufbauen und grundlegende KI-Programmierung verstehen.
Die praktische Migration zeigen wir anhand von Microsoft SQL Server – auf Wunsch unterstützen wir Sie selbstverständlich auch bei der Migration auf weitere Systeme wie PostgreSQL oder MySQL.

Sichern Sie sich jetzt Ihr Ticket unter:
👉 https://appfact.de/konferenz/tickets

entry.title: Re: Special stock returns
entry.content:
Not clear what do you mean by Special Stock. Since in sales side, multiple special stock indicators are there, mention which one you are using.
Now coming to inventory value of returns, in standard, you have 651 movement type against which, the stock would be allocated to "Blocked Stock" thereby, there wont be any inventory impact. QM team can either take the inventory or reject it based on which, inventory value will get updated

entry.content:
Jesus christ.  I just want to find this note in support.  SAP is impossible for the casual user.  Unbelievable.

List of notes, please.  How hard can it be?

entry.title: Re: Activity-Related Cost Planning in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hello

Thx for details presentation.

I would have thought, in the import file, to see quantities and activity types, without amount value. And then, during the import file action, SAP would have multiplied the quantities per price (A = P*M) by CC/Activity  types from KP26 or Fiori app Manage Cost Rates – Plan. Is that not possible?

In your example, you load both quantities and amount value .... 

Regards

Gerald

entry.title: Re: BP Sync issue with systemID

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud: Custom Widget & Widget Add-Ons Samples Preview

Daniel

entry.title: Re: Need another field in "Manage Customer Line Items" or "Manage Supplier Line Items
entry.content:
Dear @Sijin_Chandran ,

After adding the fields via Data Source Extension, you can use the fields in the app without any UI adaption. To display the added fields, use the View Settings dialog of the table in the app, search for the name of the added field, and select the field to add it to the table view layout.

Best regards
Steffen

entry.title: Help with Adding Attachments to Purchase Requisition in SAP Build App
entry.content:
Hi All,

I'm developing an app using SAP Build Apps to create a Purchase Requisition, and I need to add multiple attachments to the Purchase Requisition Item using the API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV API.

I have a few questions regarding this:

  1. In SAP Build, it seems this can be achieved in two ways:

    • Using a Data Variable and the Create Record function
    • Using an HTTP request

    The Create Record function seems to be the prober way. However, I keep encountering the error: "Attachment name cannot be empty." The issue is that there is no field explicitly named "Attachment Name" in the API.
    Should this instead be handled via an HTTP request?

  2. SAP Build provides a "Convert file to base64" function, but the output appears to be an unreadable base64 string. I’ve tested it with multiple online base64 decoders, but none of them can process it correctly. Is any additional modification required before sending it to S/4HANA?

I would really appreciate any guidance on resolving these issues. If anyone has a working example of this implementation, that would be extremely helpful.

Thanks in advance for your support!

Best regards,
Machiel

entry.title: The Gold Standard for record-to-report with SAP and SAP financial close solutions by BlackLine
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, Finance departments face numerous challenges in their record-to-report processes.

A framework that is often used when teams are thinking about optimizing their functions is to consider people, processes, and technology.

People – CFOs are facing significant talent shortages – and young entrants into the workforce expect more modern working environments

Process – multiple process variants, lack of standardization, and reliance on manual steps leads to error prone processes and inaccurate data, eroding confidence

Technology – over-customization has led to technical debt, and disparate data sources leads to expensive integration and high reconciliation efforts – an expensive set of solutions

As companies grow and evolve, the complexity of financial reporting increases, demanding more robust systems and processes and empowered teams. This blog will explore some of these challenges and offer insights into how Finance departments can overcome them to achieve a gold-standard record to report process.

The numerous challenges in the record to report process include significant talent shortages, inaccurate enterprise data, and costly solutions. Often finance teams struggle with dealing with multiple sources of transactions and manual processes. And the set of systems isn’t static and may evolve over the years.

Enter the "Gold Standard" for record-to-report, a suite of solutions that will set your team up for success. The Gold Standard is comprised of SAP solutions and SAP financial close solutions by BlackLine - that work together seamlessly to deliver a great experience for your finance team.

The SAP financial close solutions by BlackLine provide unique capabilities that have been premium qualified to SAP product standards and with integration scenario testing to confirm interoperability with SAP solutions. These addons support the following use cases:

  • Journal entry: automatically create and post journals to SAP S/4HANA, and potentially multiple other ERP systems, using standardized templates and workflow processes to prevent errors; substantiation documentation is automatically included.
  • Transaction matching: ingest detailed data from multiple sources, automatically matching transactions and eliminating error-prone manual handling with logic that automatically identifies repeat exceptions.
  • Financial review: review financial statements and conduct variance analysis efficiently at the entity or group levels with clearly defined ownership of explanations, reviews, and control certifications.
  • Substantiation: automate the load of reconciliation-supporting items and increase efficiency with auto-reconciliation rules and standardized reconciliation templates.
  • Intercompany: deploy a proactively managed intercompany process, including templates for origination of non-trade transactions, reconciliation, netting, and settlement.

Data is then further processed via SAP Group Reporting and financial reporting tools. Additional visibility and intelligent orchestration of the entire process is provided by SAP Advanced Financial Closing.

These use cases combine creating the gold standard –  an improved architecture that connects and unifies people, processes, and technology. Labor-intensive financial close and compliance processes are automated, routine manual tasks are reduced or eliminated, and visibility and standardization are improved. Intelligent workflows help accounting and finance teams focus on high-risk areas and support ongoing business strategies and policies.

 

Proven Business Value 

What are the positive effects companies that have adopted the gold standard approach as a part of their digital transformation? SAP and BlackLine have co-sponsored a report quantifying these benefits*:

  • People – 45% fewer days required for journal entry completion
  • Process – 25% improved reporting accuracy
  • Technology – 621% ROI with excellent project payback

 *Source: IDC Business Value Snapshot, sponsored by SAP and BlackLine, The Business Value of SAP Financial Close Solutions by BlackLine, doc #US53204425, Feb 2025.

When should finance teams think about adopting the gold standard?

The SAP financial close solutions are a set of capabilities that can be deployed regardless of where your finance team is in their landscape transformation:

  • If you don’t plan to move in the next years? That’s fine – you can use standard integration to SAP ECC or older releases and your team can benefit from automation and best-practice approaches to accounting operations already. When you’re ready to move forward one day, you’ll already be in a good position.

Customer Success Examples:

"Allowed upfront introduction of a best-in-class process technology which in turn drove standardization and improved controls."

– Consumer products customer

 

"The SAP financial close solutions by BlackLine have improved control, compliance, and the standardization of intercompany processes across a large organization with over 800 legal entities and a complex intercompany environment across multiple ERPs. The solutions provided efficient workflows, automated postings, and documented approvals."

- Professional services customer 

  

  • If you’re moving to RISE? Great! By automating manual tasks, you can lessen the impact of the close on your finance teams and give them more time to support the transformation. You will also have a “safe haven” for your substantiation details in BlackLine during the transition.

"Enabled tremendous scalability of accounting resources and activities."

- Healthcare customer

"Enabled seamless integration with SAP software, allowing the streamlining of all closing tasks performed."

– Consumer products customer

"The SAP financial close solutions by BlackLine were implemented in advance of the move to SAP S/4HANA enabling a control environment that validated the results of account balances migrated from the old ledger to the new."

– Financial services customer

 

  • If you’ve already reached your target architecture? Congratulations! Your team can still benefit from AI innovations and the additional control provided by solutions for financial close in a highly automated approach. 

"Increased efficiency through automation and had the additional benefit of leading to greater accuracy because fewer manual error-prone processes were needed. They also saved this customer a lot of rework."

– Oil & Gas customer

"Provided a holistic solution completely complimentary to SAP S/4HANA. The solution extended beyond account reconciliations and was managed by the Finance team."

– Consumer products customer

 

Want to learn more?  You can read more at sap.com or get in touch with me!

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - upload files to SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX) Service with camera
entry.content: Thanks so much, this was very helpful! This is a textbook example of how a clear and concise blog should be written.

entry.title: Re: Setting Up Programs in the LMS
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

Is it possible to modify the program type when the program is already created? I have created and assigned a program as open-ended type but now I want to switch to duration-based.

Regards,

Salomé

entry.title: Safeguard your Sales and Service Cloud (Version 2) go-live with SAP Enterprise Support!
entry.content:
SAP Enterprise Support is offering a new Continuous Quality Check service through the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC) :

Deployment Readiness for SAP Sales and Service Cloud, Version 2 (Pilot Program with selected customers)

This service is intended to perform some configuration checks and ensure that customers are going live following SAP guidelines.

Why: safeguard customer go-live by checking that customer configuration is aligned with SAP guidelines (foundational checks of customer’s system before going live in production).

What: offer a Deployment Readiness Check service (Continuous Quality Check) that provides customers with a pre-golive SAP guidelines review for key settings, parameters and general configuration guidance.

When: the service should be positioned after the technical go-live and before (4-5 weeks) the actual productive usage go live. We are targeting customers with ideally a go live end of April/May, for a remote session around end of March/beginning of April.

Prerequisites:

  • System access for SAP provided
  • Go-live date confirmed (not live yet)
  • Technical contact available on customer side to collaborate and provide feedback throughout the engagement

Deliverables:

  • During the pilot service delivery an SAP Service Engineer would connect to customer system to perform a remote analysis of key system configuration.
  • Customer will then receive a detailed report of findings, including guidelines, and an action plan for recommendations.

The intention is that via delivery of this service we can enable a smooth go-live and ensure avoidance of common pitfalls.

More information: short video here

Participation: to register you interest, please contact us via email: esac@sap.com

Let us know your location, rough details of your project timeline, and state your interest in this pilot workstream.

To find out more about the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council in general visit the website. Details of this workstream will soon be included on that page.

entry.title: Re: Microsoft Drive support in SAP BusinessObjects 4.3 SP03 Release
entry.content:
Bonjour,
Nous avons configuré via la CMC le serveur d'authorisation pour accéder aux sources de données Cloud SharePoint via Webi (BusinessObjects 4.3 SP4).
Cette configuration, nous permet d'accéder au OneDrive de l'utilisateur mais pas aux fichiers partagés à l'utilisateurs via le site SharePoint de l'entreprise.
Nous n'avons pas trouvé de documentation précise sur l'accès au SharePoint.
Quelqu’un a-t-il des éléments sur l'accès aux données partagées via SharePoint?
En vous remerciant par avance.
Belle journée

entry.title: Malaysian bank account number configuration in SAP ECC6 / S4HANA
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Can you please confirm, SAP don't allow longer bank account formats for Malaysia more than 18 characters ? 

 

Thanks

entry.title: How to add REST API Data Entity for Service Cloud V2?
entry.content:
Hello together,
Being very new to SAP world and Build Apps, I am not able to fetch the entities of SCV2. The use case to fetch "location" data inside Build Apps from SCV2 using Basic Authentication method. Works very well in Postman. https://api.sap.com/api/SalesSvcCloudV2_functionalLocation/overview

Created destination with steps mentioned here. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/sap-systems#adding-a-btp-destination-prerequisites


Destination additional properties:

Destination was added under integration tab.

After many trial and error, I still get following error message and cannot list expected data inside Build Apps.


After "Run Test", I got following response, but no expected response again.

What wrong I am doing?

Can anyone support please? Thanks.

@Dan_Wroblewski

entry.title: Re: Variance delta symbol
entry.content: Hello, can I ask how you displyed the delta symbol in the first place? this is not the default display layout, no?

entry.title: Re: Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025
entry.content:
@r_poelstra , @ayman_salem Hi, the information I received is that the process for requesting a BI 2025 license key is described in the following SAP note : https://me.sap.com/notes/0002251998. 

Regarding the emergency key, could you please indicate where you obtained it? Maybe there is a support email I could use to request a change.

entry.title: Blank Page at End of Subreport
entry.content:
Good morning,

I have a report into which I am adding a subreport.  The subreport consists of multiple detail sections and at various points, the detail section is marked as "Print at Bottom of Page".  Doing so, gives me a subreport with 3 pages.

Now, the last detail section also has Print at Bottom on Page set.  

**** NOTE - the page footer and report footer are suppressed.

When the subreport is imported into the main report, the subreport ends up producing an extra page.

I've ensured that the section we are adding into also is set to not Add a break after.

What can I be missing?  If I disable the last detail section in the subreport, it will not produce the blank page.

Thanks,

- Nick

entry.title: Tile: P&L Account Closing Posting - China (IDCNPLFWD) - How to post without FB01 Access.
entry.content:
We are using S/4HANA. We have a GUI Tile "P&L Account Closing Posting - China (IDCNPLFWD). It is failing as the user does not have access to FB01.

Our controls mean we do not want users to be able to post financial documents directly.

How can we get this tile to work without direct access to FB01?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: REFX Infinite Building (perpetual term): contract master data & postings (public cloud)
entry.content: @HenrikeGrötecke, @anja_wolf-galka , would you have any feedback on my latest comment? Many thanks, Cedric

entry.title: Re: Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025
entry.content:
https://support.sap.com/en/my-support/keys.html

This is the page for downloading emergency keys.  And read the note:

Note: Analytics Solutions from SAP have emergency License Keys that you can use while your permanent license key is being processed.

The pdf only has keys for 4.3.

As for the link to the sap note, we are aware of this but BI 2025 isn't available in the the drop down when trying to add a system

entry.title: Re: How to Add a Custom Discount field in Sales order Fro...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can use the condition fast entry concept to apply discounts to the sales order item section, eliminating the need for custom development.

 

Select the Pricing procedure determination.

 

Select the discount condition type in the condition type for fast entry.

 

In the sales order line item level it will be visible.

 

Regards,
Chathuranga Rajapaksha.

entry.title: Re: Excited to be part of the community;.
entry.content: Welcome @Zayn07 - great to have you in SAP Community. Where are you based and have you already checked out our location groups?

entry.title: Re: Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025
entry.content: Sorry. Posted twice by accident

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward - Sandra Solís
entry.content: @Sandra_Solis you are wonderful to work with and a great inspiration. You bring FUN to tech. I look forward to see what is next for you!

entry.title: Re: Hi there, I have a challenge while integrating SAP JC...
entry.content: Well, to make a long story short: JCo does not support Maven projects. And it cannot easily be achieved, because JCo is not a pure Java application, but a mixed application with Java and C/C++ parts, which requires the platform-dependent C/C++ library (sapjco3.dll / libsapjco3.so) to be present in the "correct" location and added to the java.library.path.

entry.title: Sales rebate agreement for a single customer based on invoices of multiple customers
entry.content:
Hello,

Is it possible to do some custom development in the condition contract management process (or is there another option), so that a sales rebate agreement for a single customer is used to issue a credit note/memo based on invoices of other multiple customers, considering that there are no sales to this customer for which the agreement is created, but a credit note has to be issued to this customer based on the other customer's invoices. There is a connection between this customer and the other customers, outside SAP, which require this.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP GENAI Deployment URL is not able to connect in PO...
entry.content:
Hi @mishra_mayank ,

Yes, it got solved after getting the right client id and secret information. 

Regards,

Padmindra

entry.title: Re: How to use the Libraries effectively in SAP Cloud ALM?
entry.content:
Dear Bruno,

Thank you for your feedback regarding libraries in SAP Cloud ALM! It’s great to see interest in making the best use of the available functionality, and I’d be happy to clarify some of your points.

1. The Current State of Libraries in SAP Cloud ALM

We are starting with a very basic concept of libraries in SAP Cloud ALM. The initial focus is a minimal viable product to support the Selective Data Transfer (SDT) from SAP Solution Manager and lay the foundation for reusable elements in Cloud ALM. Since this is an evolving feature, additional functionality, best practices, and enhanced documentation will be provided as we extend the capabilities.

2. Finding the Libraries App

Currently, the Libraries application is not prominently featured (due to point 1) in the standard navigation of the Implementation sections. However, it can be accessed via the search bar in the SAP Cloud ALM Launchpad. This will be improved as we enhance the user experience and refine the integration of libraries across Cloud ALM functionalities. 
We recognize also the need for general more guidance on Cloud ALM integration and will provide more structured documentation soon.

3. Reusing Library Elements Across Projects

At this stage, library elements serve as centralized, reusable elements with a focus on system groups (landscape) rather than projects. Your suggestion of adding a library element to a project while preserving its associated documents, test cases, and related elements aligns well with our roadmap. We aim to enable broader reuse and improve integration with other Cloud ALM functionalities in future updates.

4. Next Steps and Future Enhancements

We appreciate your insights, and as more functionalities become available, we will publish best practice guides and improved UI/UX enhancements to make the process more intuitive. We are also working on further enhancing relations and dependencies, ensuring that library elements can be fully leveraged across implementation.

For now, we encourage you to keep exploring and sharing your feedback—it helps shape the evolution of Cloud ALM to better meet user needs.

P.S. There is a Customer Council Call for Libraries soon
https://workzone.one.int.sap/site#workzone-home&/groups/ltiUgwDg3YWXOk40QNk7rS/events/WDn2qcbEs6KmWswxwK54rJ

entry.title: Re: Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025
entry.content: @GregBotticchioFurthermore, the note you mentioned has not been updated to include BI 2025

entry.title: 'Modify' stmt showing sy-subrc = 0 for duplicate record
entry.content:
Hi,
In my prog, I am updating a Z*table using Modify from a workarea stmt within a loop..endloop.
The work area is exactly like DBtable structure with 4 unique keys. I have 4 keys in a Z*Table.

I have a Modify Z*INVDBTAB from wa_invdoc stmt. Modify is showing sy-subrc = 0 even when it encounters a duplicate record with same set of unique keys but fortunately the Z*DB table is NOT updating that duplicate record(which is correct).

The Modify stsmt should show sy-subrc = 4 right? 

Can someone Pls clarify.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP FinOps Application as a SaaS offering ?
entry.content: My thoughts... I wish it would be included in the sap for me cockpit before becoming a SaaS customers have to pay for and then perhaps stand up (if BTP Subaccount Saas).

entry.title: Re: How to Configure Remote OS Script Monitoring in Solution Manager 7.1 and 7.2
entry.content:
HI All,

Upon verifying internally, from SAP Solution Manager 7.2 version

3132826 - OS Command Console in SAP Solution Manager

*****    For security reasons the OS Command Console has been removed from SAP Solution Manager .

In fact , the RCA tools( including OS Command Console )  have been decommissioned from SAP Solution Manager for  security purposes.

 

For this case how to create and maintain the custom script , Kindly help with step by step process

entry.title: Re: No Identity Provisioning on IAS
entry.content: Does this note help? https://me.sap.com/notes/0003521979

entry.title: Re: "Mastering the Application Rationalization with SAP L...
entry.content: Hi Omar, thanks for your quick reply. I have no problem to access to my learning assets with my universal ID. But the mentioned training provides a specific link to access LeanIX, an a specific user-id and password. When you enter the link and provide the credentials, you get invalid user-id or password, meaning that the provided credentials to the training environment are not valid. I hope this clarifies it. Kind regards,

entry.title: Sub assembly showing production order.
entry.content:
Good afternoon everyone, I have a query that no one in our team has an answer for.

We are using sub assembly ICC within a production BoM, highlighted below:

I am looking to see if it possible to have the components contained within this combo code then displayed on the production order, like a breakdown on sublines, below is screen shot on these components:

The reason for us looking to do this is to prevent us having to always update the long text contained within the routing of the IPC when the sub assembly gets updated with new materials.

 

Thanks

Stephen

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud Customizing Purchase contract expiry status transition
entry.content:
Dear experts,

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, the validity status of a purchase contrat changes to "Expiring" 30 days before its end validity date.

My customer requires the ability to modify this behavior and customize the timeframe from when the statut moves from "valid" to "expiring". For example:

Contract 1 moves to "expiring" status 90 days before the end validity date

Contract 2 moves to "expiring" status 60 days before the end validity date.

Could you please confirm if it is possible to adjust this setting? If so, what would be the recommended approach to configure it?

Thank you for your support

entry.title: Is there a project plan for integrating Solution Manager ITSM with GRC?
entry.content:
It has been many years (maybe decades) since I was asked to do this and much of my documentation has been destroyed.

Does anyone have a project plan to integrate Solution Manager 7.2 with GRC 12.0?

I'll keep looking in what's left of my own, and elsewhere online, but appreciate any help.

Thank you.

Olá Otávio

Atenciosamente,

Guilherme Zenzen

entry.title: Re: No Identity Provisioning on IAS
entry.content:
This note is no help https://me.sap.com/notes/0003521979
I have an IPS issue on IAS.

entry.title: Are necessary to stop ASE server and replication in order to DUMP and DISK RESIZE in ASE?
entry.content:
Issue : 

 

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 

ASE @Site1
##
00:0003:00000:00025:2024/10/29 22:43:08.03 server  Error: 1105, Severity: 17, State: 2
00:0003:00000:00025:2024/10/29 22:43:08.08 server  Can't allocate space for object 'js_output' in database 'sybmgmtdb' because 'default' segment is full/has no free extents. If you ran out of space in syslogs, dump the transaction log. Otherwise, use ALTER DATABASE to increase the size of the segment.
...
00:0003:00000:00019:2025/03/12 14:45:03.83 server  Error: 1105, Severity: 17, State: 2
00:0003:00000:00019:2025/03/12 14:45:03.83 server  Can't allocate space for object 'js_history' in database 'sybmgmtdb' because 'default' segment is full/has no free extents. If you ran out of space in syslogs, dump the transaction log. Otherwise, use ALTER DATABASE to increase the size of the segment.
00:0003:00000:00019:2025/03/12 14:45:03.83 kernel  Job Scheduler (js__exec_handler): Exception 3602 raised.
00:0003:00000:00019:2025/03/12 14:45:03.83 kernel  Job Scheduler got exception for execution token 1.
##

 

Our actions for this problem: 

 

1.Take the backup of sybmgmtdb database in primary Site1 node.

 

dump database sybmgmtdb to "/path/sybmgmtdb.dmp"

 

If we run it when primary DB, application and Replication is online and running then should it be a problem ?

 

OR 

 

2.

 

Check database current log devices:

 

sp_helpdb <SID>
go

 

To resize an existing log device:

 

use master
go
disk resize name = '<existing_log_device_name>', size = '<size in GB>G'  -- size is what is being added, not the total target size of the device
go
alter database <dbname> log on <existing_log_device_name> = '<size in GB>G' 
go

 

If we run it when primary DB, application and Replication is online and running then should it be a problem ?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Are necessary to stop ASE server and replication in o...
entry.content:
Dear customer, 

Regarding to this error, it is important to consider that the DEFAULT segment is a DATA one, not a LOG one. 

About the other requests:

No, it are not necessary to stop the replication and ASE server (such as the disk resize are executed into ISQL connection). 

We just have an advise about the disk resize with replication server:

  1. First, double check that there is enough space to increase the size.
  2. It is recommended to increase the same amount on the device in the other site of replication.

Here is a document about the disk resize: 

disk resize | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: SAML and 2212795
entry.content: The current SAML inplementation on BOBJ only supports 1 URL so itr must be either HTTPS or HTTP. Supporting multiple URL's must be added as an enhancement, or by using multiple web/apps (each can be it's own URL)

entry.title: Adding Successors Column to Calibration Session
entry.content:
Hello,

Our client wants to be able to add/edit succession nominations within the calibration list view. Instead of opening the Talent Card, they want an additional column. It should be editable like the Talent Flag columns.

Does anyone know how I can add that?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Remote Freelance SAP Job Opportunities | Romania
entry.content:
🚀 Join a Global Project as an SAP Consultant! 🚀

We’re hiring an experienced SAP CO, SAP FI, SAP IBP, SAP PP Consultant for a long-term, remote B2B freelance roles in #Romania with a leading global client. If you’re skilled in SAP Modules and want to work on impactful projects, this is your opportunity!

🔹 Role: SAP CO , FI, IBP, PP Consultants 
🔹 Location: #Remote (Romania)
🔹 Engagement: #B2B #Freelance

What We’re Looking For:
✅ Strong expertise in SAP CO / FI / PP / IBP
✅ Ability to work independently in a remote setup
✅ Fluent English & excellent communication skills

💼 Interested? Send your CV to 📩 sagar.r@dabster.net or #refer a consultant in your #network!

#SAPCO #SAPIBP #SAPPP #SAPConsultant #FreelanceOpportunity #RemoteJob #SAPJobs #B2B #Hiring

entry.content: Very useful blog, as usual. Thanks you @christian_ah-soon 👍

entry.title: Re: Get started with the ABAP custom code migration process
entry.content:
@BaerbelWinkler,

Hi Bärbel,

it is always good to have the central ATC system on the newest release :-). Actually I don't expect, that the 2021 release could be a problem for the scoping based on SUSG snapshots. This functionality was implemented a long time ago and should be stable. I would rather suspect, that something is wrong with the SUSG snapshot(s).

Best regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: #CoE#LAL Cannot create sales order with customer down...
entry.content:
Hello,

free goods are not invoice-relevant, as they do not generate any payment obligations. In contrast, a down payment requires billing and revenue recognition. Therefore, receiving an advance payment for items that do not require payment is not feasible.

entry.title: how to format date in Fiori elements odata V2 ?
entry.content:
Hello Experts!

I havbe odata field type DateTime.

It is used in following contexts:

  1. .Filtering on list report
  2. data entry on objecdt page.

I would like to format it so that it displays only month and year. 
I cannot find resource on how to do it in XML annotation - my backend is SEGW based so i cannot use CDS.

Anmy advice on how to do it ?
